item.title: Saturday WHOLESOME Dump To Improve Your Mood #319
item.description:
1. That is one chill cat
2. Insert happy feelings
3. Doggo about to fly
4. Why have you forsaken me, Mother?!
5. The Most Nonchalant Ace Combat Pilot
6. Big Ears Does This When She Wants To Be Picked Up
7. Horse mimics owner who is making Snow Angels
8. God Forbid Girls Have Some Fun
9. Wholesome Packaging
10.
11. Awww
12. Have You Had The Brosnan Latte Yet? I hear it's good
13. Jack Black Should Play A SuperHero Dad?
14. A Good Way To Wake Up
15. Hey you, have a good day ;) P.S. For the past 20 months, I’ve been working on my own game, and while it’s starting to get a bit of attention, it just makes me doubt myself even more. A lot of people have played the demo, and I can feel the pressure because, come release day, it’s all or nothing. Some days it feels great, and other days, I’m completely burnt out. I just hope I can give people a similar experience to the ones I had growing up, playing games and having a blast. If you’d like to support my efforts, a simple wishlist would mean the world to me: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_Or_Youll_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S.+ Have an awesome Weekend!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

.

.
And I love them.
Daily.
.

No.
.
.
.
.

.
.
.
Heh.
.
Humor me?
Cooper Tax! Have a great weekend!

item.title: Which one of you painted this?
item.description: We later found out that one of my husband's friends commissioned it and didn't say anything or leave a note, but imagine getting this out of the blue and being so confused.
item.dc:creator: SmileDocCharlotte

item.title: BEST Showrooms Were Insane
item.description:
Best Products Company was a catalog seller with over 169 showrooms across America at their peak
The company was founded in 1957 and by 1970 decided to partner with the SITE architectural collective to create 9 showstopper locations, each with a separate unique and wildly improbably design.
The tilting facade that appears to be floating away, lifted off by the wind
Here's a model of one of the stores with the face ripped off and a jungle growing in between the body and the entrance
And here's what it actually looked like in practice
A view from the jungle in between the two pieces
My personal favorite, this one with the bricks peeling off like a sticker whose glue has dried out.
This poor crumbling ruin was designed to look post apocalyptic
There's even a section of the retail store that's been incorporated into the exterior design, with all the products whitewashed and bonded in place.
Another architectural model
And the finished product
More on the crumbling design
Including the bricks
Even the ones that weren't built by SITE were still very design heavy
The company folded in 1997, pushed out of business by the rise of Wal Mart & Target
item.dc:creator: Jonson71

item.title: When Allyson Felix became pregnant,N¡ke was prepared to cut the terms of Felix's endorsement by as much as 70% due to the pregnancy, and told her to “know your place and just run.” Amid all of this, she was forced to have an emergency c-section
item.description: When Allyson Felix became pregnant, N¡ke was prepared to cut the terms of Felix's endorsement by as much as 70% due to the pregnancy, and told her to “know your place and just run.” ? Amid all of this, she was forced to have an emergency c-section seven months into her pregnancy because of a potentially life-threatening condition and her baby had to live for more than a month in the NICU. But two years later she’s qualified for her fifth Olympics with her daughter watching. Felix dropped N¡ke and created her own brand of running shoes, the Saysh One. She used running in the Olympics in Japan wearing them under the banner “I Know My Place”. With 11 medals now, she passed Carl Lewis this summer for the most track and field medals by an American in history! "I used my voice and built this company for you. So that you never have to train at 4:30am while you're 5 months pregnant to hide your pregnancy from your sponsor.”
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Well... At least I did *something*...
item.description: Went protesting today, Omas gegen Rechts (Grannies against Rightwing) had organized it against the AfD, but given the last few days, it also became one against the CDU, too. 20k-25k people came. Can't say I enjoyed it, can't say that I believe that it will have any noticeable effect, but, well, at least I added 1 to the number of people there.

item.title: Losing my work bestie
item.description: I am going to lose my work bestie in April, possibly forever. I already lost my last work bestie which totally sucks. Teaching can be super isolating and having co-worker friends means a lot. At the same time, leaving your baby after 12 weeks? Of course that's hard. Not getting any income for 3 months? Way harder. It's impossible for a single teacher, and even if your kids are older, we don't get much in the way of sick days. This is ridiculous. The majority of teachers are female, you'd think we'd have accomodations but no. Let's churn and burn. Florida sucks.
item.dc:creator: AranaDiscoteca

item.title: Pilot Debrief - American Airlines crash in Washington DC
item.description:
American Airlines flight 5342, a CRJ-700 aircraft, collided with a UH-60 Blackhawk helicopter (US Army) on approach to Reagan International airport. There has been a ton of tragically faulty speculation by much of the news media, with only some being more prudent and cautious about presumptions (MSNBC). I won't even mention the travesty of political leadership on this (you know who I'm talking about), who made wild speculations and assumptions that are flat out wrong. YouTube channel "Pilot Debrief" is run by a gentleman named Hoover, who is an aviation expert with many years of flight experience and performing analyses of aircraft mishaps. I was waiting for his analytical video and it came faster than expected. Hoover needs to be an aviation expert on news programs. He's objective, methodical, cautious, scientific, and highly analytical. He uses REAL DATA to make LOGICAL CONCLUSIONS about the reasons for aircraft mishaps. When he doesn't know enough, he says so. He is only after the truth, not grandstanding or sensational speculation. I've broken up his video into segments purely for the convenience of Imgur viewers, as a means of helping to spread this valuable information. See FULL VIDEO HERE: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RzQe6W7vcu4
AA 5342 was on approach to RWY 33 when the UH-60 collided with it.
HOW IT BEGAN: Reagan National Airport has some of the busiest airspace in the country, but also the most restricted. It manages a lot of fixed wing & rotary aircraft.
Airport Surface Detection Equipment details. AA 5342 was on its 5th flight of the day.
Very dense fixed wing traffic approach to Reagan International. AA 5342 was inserted into the queue for landing, initially on RWY 01.
AA 5342 (CRJ7) inserted into the queue rather closely to the preceding aircraft. ATC requested flight 5307 to divert to RWY 33 for safer spacing. They declined.
AA 5342 is contacted by ATC with request to divert to RWY 33, and they accept.
AA 5342 angles to their right breaking off the RWY 01 approach to line up for landing at RWY 33. Meanwhile the rest of traffic is approaching RWY 01.
The UH-60 helicopter, PAT25, is traveling south bound along the Potomac river.
ATC communicates with PAT25 (UH-60) and informs them of the CRJ7 approaching RWY 33. PAT25 confirms visual and accepts visual separation responsibility.
CRJ7 continues approach to RWY 33. They are committed and have priority. But PAT25 (UH-60) is continuing their flight path towards the CRJ7.
ATC contacts PAT25 to confirm they have visual on the CRJ7. PAT25 is warned to get behind the CRJ7. But it appears they do not have a proper visual. They fail to avoid the aircraft and the collision results.
Who was at fault? Ultimately ATC is advisory. They have no physical means of altering aircraft trajectories. The ultimate responsibility falls upon the pilots. The UH-60 (PAT25) failed to keep an adequate visual on the closest proximity aircraft. ATC warned them of the CRJ7 approach and to get behind them. The UH-60 failed to comply. Keep in mind, the danger zone of collision was short and rapid. And too much reliance upon technology means its deficiencies can cause faulty presumptions. If the CA equipment on board the UH-60 had greater range, perhaps they could have altered course in time to avoid the collision. One other consideration is ATC. We did not hear them exclaim "PROXIMITY ALERT" to PAT25. They were presuming PAT25 had a visual on the CRJ7, so the command was to "pass behind." The CRJ7 was committed and could not alter course. It was up to the UH-60 to change course, as they are far more maneuverable. They can halt forward motion and hover. They can rapidly descend or ascend. According to some claims offered up in the news, ATC may have been undermanned. Normally there is one controller dedicated to monitoring and controlling helicopters. In this instance, one controller was managing both fixed wing and rotary aircraft. This may have also diminished necessary attention to help ensure this collision did not occur. And lastly, we have the further complication of night time, in a very dense environment with numerous light sources on the ground. Anytime aircraft are flying low in these circumstances, other aircraft can have difficulty with visual confirmation of other aircraft due to relative movements of aircraft lights against the backdrop of numerous lights on the ground. A big THANK YOU to Hoover of Pilot Debrief for his tremendously insightful and useful analysis of this aircraft mishap. There's still plenty of data not yet on hand that will help with a final determination. But given what has been provided thus far, we have a pretty reasonable understanding of what happened and hopefully this will lead to actions that will help prevent this kind of mishap from happening again.
item.dc:creator: cytherians
